unit: one scheduled fresh-session generation or judge call
latency_stop: provider subprocess return, timeout, or transport exception
retry_policy: report first-attempt latency, retry latency, total latency, and retry count separately; do not replace failed first attempts with successful retry latency
cache_policy: fresh provider session for every call; no application-level response cache; provider-side caching is recorded when reported and otherwise unknown
rate_limit_policy: retain rate-limit failures and waiting time in transport metadata; report chronological/vendor sensitivity and do not impute counterfactual latency
tokens: use provider-returned input/output token counts only; unavailable counts are NA and are not estimated from text
monetary_cost: derive only from provider-returned billable usage and a frozen price table dated before the run; otherwise report NA
aggregation: paired within record and originating vendor; failures, retries, and missing usage remain separately visible
